articles.title: Past Savings Accepted: Small Cash Introduction Cannot Be Treated as Unexplained
articles.summary: An Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that cash introduced into a taxpayer's capital account cannot be treated as unexplained if supported by past savings and financial records. The tribunal accepted evidence from the assessee's balance sheet and financial history, emphasizing the need for practical evaluation of a taxpayer's capacity. This decision impacts how tax authorities assess unexplained cash deposits in future cases. The ruling was delivered in the context of an income tax dispute.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that cash introduced into capital cannot be treated as unexplained when supported by past savings. It accepted the assessees financial history and balance sheet evidence. The ruling emphasizes practical evaluation of taxpayer capacity. The post Past Savings Accepted: Small Cash Introduction Cannot Be Treated as Unexplained appeared first on TaxGuru .

In this case, the Assessing Officer assessed total cash deposits instead of only demonetization-related deposits, leading to an invalid addition. The tribunal deleted the addition, citing a procedural breach. The ruling impacts how tax authorities conduct limited scrutiny assessments in future cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer cannot examine issues beyond the scope of limited scrutiny without approval. articles.title: Reopening on Change of Opinion Invalid: ITAT Quashes Entire Assessment
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed reassessment proceedings initiated by tax authorities, ruling that a mere change of opinion without fresh evidence does not justify reopening cases under section 147 of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal emphasized that re-examining already scrutinized facts is insufficient for reassessment. This decision impacts tax administration and reassessment practices in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal quashed reassessment proceedings as they were based on a mere change of opinion without any fresh tangible material. It held that re-examining already scrutinized facts does not justify reopening under section 147. articles.title: Unexplained Jewellery Investment addition Upheld as Refund Credited in Assessee’s Account
articles.summary: An Income Tax Tribunal upheld an addition to an assessee's income for unexplained jewellery investments after finding that payments and delivery were controlled by the assessee’s personal assistant. The tribunal rejected the assessee’s claim that the jewellery was purchased by their daughter due to lack of evidence. The refund related to the addition was credited to the assessee’s account. The ruling reinforces tax compliance and documentation requirements under India’s income tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that payments and delivery handled by the assessee’s PA indicated control over the transaction. The absence of evidence supporting the daughter’s role led to confirmation of addition. articles.title: Constitutional Peril Of 'Non-Speaking' Order
articles.summary: The Speaker and Chairman rejected a removal notice against Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar without providing a reasoned explanation, raising concerns about adherence to natural justice principles. The article argues that 'speaking orders'—decisions with clear reasoning—are essential for the rule of law, citing judicial precedents like Kihoto Hollohan. This rejection marks a significant departure from established norms, potentially undermining institutional transparency and accoun
articles.raw_summary: The summary rejection of the CEC removal notice by the Speaker and the Chairman—absent a reasoned explanation—marks a significant departure from the principles of natural justice. As "speaking orders" are foundational to the rule of law, presiding officers, when acting in an adjudicatory capacity, are bound by judicial precedents like Kihoto Hollohan to ensure transparency over institutional silence. articles.title: DRP 130426: CAG Reveals Governance Failure in J&K Wetlands Management
articles.summary: A CAG report reveals that the Jammu and Kashmir government's conservation efforts for wetlands were limited to just six lakes, while 691 other lakes were ignored. The Forest Department did not identify eligible water bodies or seek assistance under central schemes. This raises concerns about governance and environmental policy implementation in the region.
articles.raw_summary: According to the report, government conservation efforts were limited to only six lakes—Dal, Wular, Hokersar, Manasbal, Surinsar, and Mansar—which were examined in detail. For the remaining 691 lakes, the Forest Department neither identified eligible water bodies nor formulated plans to seek assistance under schemes of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF CC).

articles.title: Full Exemption of Leave Encashment Allowed – Government Service Character Prevails Over PSU Status
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that leave encashment for government service remains fully tax-exempt under Section 10(10AA), even if the employee is later absorbed into a Public Sector Undertaking (PSU). The decision clarifies that the nature of the benefit is tied to government service, not PSU status. This ruling impacts tax treatment for government employees transitioning to PSUs.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that leave encashment relating to government service remains fully exempt under Section 10(10AA). It ruled that later absorption into a PSU does not change the nature of the benefit. The post Full Exemption of Leave Encashment Allowed – Government Service Character Prevails Over PSU Status appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: The rise of infrastructure for digital censorshipA series of IT Rules amendments has shifted online speech control from courts to the executive, enabling fast, opaque takedowns with limited accountability.12 April, 20266 min readApar GuptaNikhil Pahwa
articles.summary: A series of amendments to India's IT Rules has transferred control over online speech from the judiciary to the executive branch. This change allows for rapid, opaque content takedowns with minimal oversight. The Internet Freedom Foundation highlights concerns over accountability and free speech implications. The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

articles.title: CIT(A) Enhancement Quashed for No Notice – ITAT Restores LTCG Issues to AO
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) set aside an income tax enhancement made by the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] because no notice was issued under section 251(2). The tribunal ruled that this violated natural justice principles, leading to the quashing of the enhancement and related penalty directions. The case involved Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) issues, which have been restored to the Assessing Officer (AO) for fresh consideration.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that enhancement of income without issuing notice under section 251(2) is invalid. Such action violates principles of natural justice, leading to quashing of enhancement and related penalty directions. The post CIT(A) Enhancement Quashed for No Notice   ITAT Restores LTCG Issues to AO appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SAD Refund Allowed as Invoice Format Differences Held Procedural, Not Substantive
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that procedural differences in invoice formats should not lead to denial of tax refunds under exemption notifications. The decision emphasizes that minor procedural lapses should not invalidate substantive benefits. This supports a practical approach to tax compliance. The ruling was made in the context of a refund claim under the SAD (Special Additional Duty) regime.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal emphasized the distinction between procedural and substantive requirements in exemption notifications. It held that procedural lapses should not result in denial of benefits. The ruling supports a pragmatic approach to compliance. articles.title: GST Rectification Order Quashed as Section 161 Conditions Not Met: Gujarat HC
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court set aside a GST rectification order issued to a taxpayer because it failed to meet the statutory conditions under Section 161 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act. The court directed tax authorities to issue a fresh order after considering the taxpayer’s reply. This ruling highlights procedural compliance requirements in tax administration under India’s GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that the rectification order failed to satisfy the statutory requirements under Section 161 of the CGST Act. The impugned order was quashed, directing issuance of a fresh speaking order after considering the taxpayer’s reply. The post GST Rectification Order Quashed as Section 161 Conditions Not Met: Gujarat HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Allahabad HC Quashes GST Order for Lack of Proper Hearing Notice
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court invalidated a GST cancellation order because the tax authority only issued the hearing notice through an online portal without physical service. The Court ruled that this violated natural justice principles, as the taxpayer was denied a proper opportunity to respond. The decision underscores the importance of due process in tax administration and could influence future GST enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The case involved issuance of notice only through the portal after cancellation of registration. The Court held that absence of physical service violated natural justice and invalidated the order. The post Allahabad HC Quashes GST Order for Lack of Proper Hearing Notice appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Vedaparai reservoir project remains a long-pending demand in Anthiyur constituency
articles.summary: The Vedaparai reservoir project in Tamil Nadu's Anthiyur constituency remains unresolved ahead of the 2026 elections. Residents in the plains seek water management solutions like check dams and water diversion to improve irrigation, but hill communities oppose these plans due to fears of ecological damage to forests, wildlife, and traditional livelihoods. The project has been a recurring demand in local politics.
articles.raw_summary: The constituency has seen several water management proposals, including check dams across the Maniyachi Pallam stream and at Valukkuparai in the hills, along with plans to divert water to the reservoir. While these measures can improve irrigation in the plains, hill residents fear ecological disruption, including impacts on forests, wildlife and traditional livelihoods.

The decision balances procedural leniency with substantive justice in tax disputes.

articles.title: FIR Quashing Petition Not Infructuous After Chargesheet Due to Wider Jurisdiction: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a writ petition for quashing an FIR does not become infructuous after the filing of a chargesheet if broader jurisdiction under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) is invoked. The decision clarifies the legal standing of such petitions, ensuring continued judicial scrutiny even post-chargesheet. This ruling impacts how criminal cases are handled and reinforces the judiciary's role in overseeing FIRs under the new legal regime.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether a writ petition for FIR quashing becomes infructuous after filing of a chargesheet. The Court held it does not, where broader jurisdiction under BNSS is invoked. articles.title: Foreign Tax Credit Cannot Be Denied for Late Form 67 – Bang ITAT Allows Substantive Relief
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Bangalore ruled that procedural delays in filing Form 67 cannot be used to deny Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) to taxpayers. The tribunal directed the Assessing Officer (AO) to grant FTC after verifying the evidence, emphasizing that procedural rules should not override substantive tax relief under Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements (DTAA). This decision underscores the primacy of DTAA provisions over procedural formalities in tax matters.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal emphasized that procedural rules like Form 67 filing timelines are not meant to deny legitimate FTC. It directed the AO to grant credit after verifying evidence. The case highlights the primacy of DTAA provisions. The post Foreign Tax Credit Cannot Be Denied for Late Form 67 – Bang ITAT Allows Substantive Relief appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Penalty Quashed as Notice Failed to Specify Exact Section 271(1)(c) Charge: ITAT Raipur
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Raipur quashed a penalty notice issued to a taxpayer because it failed to specify whether the charge was for concealment of income or furnishing inaccurate particulars under Section 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act. The Tribunal ruled that the vagueness in the notice invalidated the entire penalty proceedings. This decision highlights the importance of precise legal drafting in tax penalty notices and may influence future tax administration practices.

The Tribunal held that such vagueness invalidates the entire penalty proceedings. articles.summary: The Institute of Company Secretaries of India (ICSI) has highlighted delays by Registrars of Companies (RoCs) in marking defective annual forms under the Companies Fresh Start Scheme (CCFS) 2026. ICSI has urged the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) to either mandate time-bound processing of these forms or allow direct refiling to ease compliance challenges for companies. articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a custodian cannot be held liable for duty discrepancies in container contents unless there is evidence of tampered or broken seals. The tribunal held that estimated additions do not establish concealment of income, making the penalty unsustainable. This decision impacts tax assessment practices and could influence future tax policy interpretations.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Call to save River Bhavani echoes in Mettupalayam constituency
articles.summary: The Bhavani River, a key water source in Tamil Nadu, has nearly dried up, leaving stagnant water in small pits between Mettupalayam and Sirumugai since early March. The river supports over 20 drinking water schemes for four districts, raising concerns about water scarcity. Local voices are calling for urgent government intervention to address the crisis, especially ahead of the 2026 state elections. The situation highlights broader environmental and water management challenges in the region.
articles.raw_summary: The river, originating in the Nilgiris and flowing 216 km through four districts, has over 20 combined drinking water schemes connected to it between Pillur and Lower Bhavani (Bhavanisagar reservoir). It has almost dried up, with water stagnating in small pits between Mettupalayam town and Sirumugai from the first week of March in the ongoing summer.

articles.title: The paradox of making gender-fluid lives legible to the law
articles.summary: The article explores how India's legal system struggles to accommodate gender-fluid identities due to its reliance on fixed categories. It highlights the mismatch between transgressive identities and legal frameworks, which could lead to future policy or judicial interventions. The piece underscores the need for legal recognition of diverse gender identities in India.
articles.raw_summary: Transgressive identities resist fixed and stable categories so crucial to the legal process.

articles.title: CAAS Moves Supreme Court on ITAT Vacancies
articles.summary: The Central Administrative Appeals Secretariat (CAAS) has filed a petition in the Supreme Court regarding the significant backlog in the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) due to unfilled positions and delayed appointments. The intervention emphasizes the urgent need to operationalize approved members to restore efficiency and ensure timely justice. The Supreme Court's involvement highlights the systemic issue of judicial vacancies impacting tax dispute resolution.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns massive backlog in ITAT caused by unfilled positions and delayed appointments. The intervention highlights that immediate operationalisation of approved members is necessary to restore efficiency and timely justice. articles.title: Maharashtra To Fast-Track Land Allocation For Data Centres
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government has directed the City and Industrial Development Corporation (CIDCO) to develop a land bank to expedite land allocation for data centres. This move aims to position Maharashtra as a major hub for data infrastructure. The initiative is part of efforts to strengthen the state's digital and technological capabilities.
articles.raw_summary: The government has asked the City and Industrial Development Corporation (CIDCO) to develop a land bank to position Maharashtra as a major data infrastructure hub.

articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 387 | Deepa Joshi v Gaurav Joshi
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a spouse's primary duty to maintain their partner cannot be reduced due to loan repayments for asset creation. The case involved Deepa Joshi and Gaurav Joshi, where the court emphasized that financial obligations for shared assets do not diminish the legal responsibility to support a spouse. This judgment reinforces protections for spousal rights under family law.
articles.raw_summary: Duty To Maintain Spouse Primary; Loan Repayments For Asset Creation No Ground To Reduce Maintenance Liability: Supreme Court

articles.title: Corporate Affairs ministry proposes easing company incorporation rules
articles.summary: The Corporate Affairs Ministry is proposing to simplify company incorporation rules to boost entrepreneurship. The ministry plans to consolidate multiple forms into fewer e-forms and relax KYC and documentation requirements for subscribers. This move aims to reduce bureaucratic hurdles in starting businesses in India.
articles.raw_summary: In an effort to boost entrepreneurship, the corporate affairs ministry is poised to revamp the company incorporation process. By consolidating numerous forms into a reduced set of e-forms, the ministry aims to slash bureaucratic red tape. Expect a more lenient approach to KYC protocols and documentation for subscribers.

articles.title: Cabinet Greenlights 2% DA Hike for Central Staff and ₹24,815 Cr Rail Projects in UP & AP
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved a 2% increase in dearness allowance (DA) for central government staff and pensioners, raising the DA from 58% to 60% of basic pay/pension. Additionally, the Centre sanctioned ₹24,815 crore for railway projects in Uttar Pradesh and Andhra Pradesh. These decisions impact government finances and infrastructure development in the two states.
articles.raw_summary: The Centre on Saturday increased dearness allowance (DA) and dearness relief (DR) by two per cent, over the existing rate of 58 per cent of the basic pay/pension.

articles.title: HC refuses to quash FIR against 2 Muslim students accused of trying to convert Hindu classmate
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court refused to quash an FIR filed against two Muslim students accused of attempting to convert a Hindu classmate. The students claimed they had filed a complaint against the classmate’s brother for harassment, but the case was registered under laws related to religious conversion. The court’s decision highlights legal scrutiny of such cases involving religious and minority rights.
articles.raw_summary: The petitioners alleged that their Hindu classmate’s brother had been harassing one of them and filed the case after they complained against him.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Bhavani constituency faces infrastructure gaps, livelihood concerns
articles.summary: The Bhavani jamakkalam sector in Tamil Nadu, known for its handwoven textiles (GI-tagged in 2005), is struggling due to competition from cheaper powerloom products. Weavers in the constituency face livelihood concerns as their traditional market shrinks. The article highlights infrastructure gaps and economic challenges ahead of the 2026 state elections, with potential implications for government intervention.
articles.raw_summary: The Bhavani jamakkalam sector, despite receiving a GI tag in 2005, is struggling for survival. Weavers say proliferation of cheaper powerloom products has severely affected the market for authentic handwoven items.

articles.title: Assam: Clashes erupt between forest officials, Adivasis during eviction drive, internet suspended
articles.summary: Clashes broke out in Assam between forest officials and Adivasis during an eviction drive after 25 Adivasis were detained for allegedly encroaching on reserved forest land. Internet services were suspended in the area following the unrest. The incident highlights tensions over forest land rights and tribal displacement, which may prompt government or judicial review. The Adivasis are a historically marginalized community in India.
articles.raw_summary: The tensions erupted after 25 Adivasis were detained for allegedly encroaching on reserved forest land.

The results reflect shifting political dynamics in the region.

articles.title: Judicial Pendency: Accountability—Or Misattribution? [Part II]
articles.summary: The article examines why the narrative of judicial pendency persists in India, attributing it to the lack of a meaningful statistical framework to track case progression. It questions the link between pendency and inefficiency or corruption, suggesting deeper structural issues in the judicial system. The discussion is part of a broader analysis on accountability in India's legal framework.
articles.raw_summary: Part-2: Narrative, Capacity, and the Bypass of Law Dissection of the Narrative of Pendency The question then arises: why does the narrative of judicial pendency persist, so readily linked with inefficiency and even corruption? The answer lies not in data, but in its absence. articles.title: The PLA Deepens China’s Political Rectification Campaign
articles.summary: The People's Liberation Army (PLA) in China is intensifying its political rectification campaign. This initiative, involving the military, aims to align PLA activities with the Chinese Communist Party's ideological goals. The campaign could influence China's military posture and regional security dynamics, which may have implications for India's strategic and defence policies.

articles.title: Faster insolvency process proposed with creditor-led resolution framework
articles.summary: The government has proposed a new Creditor-initiated Insolvency Resolution Process (CIIRP) to speed up debt resolution. Financial creditors holding 51% of the debt can now initiate proceedings directly, bypassing the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) admission process. The NCLT's role is limited to approving moratoriums and plans, with the entire process aimed to be completed within 150 days, down from the current 330 days.
articles.raw_summary: A new Creditor-initiated Insolvency Resolution Process (CIIRP) aims to expedite debt resolution, allowing financial creditors with 51% debt share to initiate proceedings. This bypasses the NCLT admission process, with the tribunal's role limited to moratorium and plan approval. The entire process is targeted for completion within 150 days, a significant reduction from the current 330 days.

articles.title: ITC Denied on Lease Rentals as Land Used for Factory Construction: AAAR Gujarat
articles.summary: The Gujarat Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAAR) ruled that businesses cannot claim Input Tax Credit (ITC) on Goods and Services Tax (GST) paid for lease rentals when the land is used for constructing a factory. The authority cited Section 17(5)(d) of the GST law, which explicitly blocks such credit regardless of timing or stage of usage. The ruling applies to ITC claims related to land lease rentals used for industrial construction.
articles.raw_summary: The AAAR held that GST paid on lease rentals for land used to construct a factory is not eligible for ITC. It ruled that Section 17(5)(d) clearly blocks such credit irrespective of timing or usage stage. The post ITC Denied on Lease Rentals as Land Used for Factory Construction: AAAR Gujarat appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz: Iran and the US disagree on international law governing the sea
articles.summary: The U.S. and Iran are in disagreement over the legal status of the Strait of Hormuz. Washington considers it an international waterway, while Tehran claims it as part of its territorial waters. The dispute highlights tensions over maritime rights and could impact global oil shipping routes, which are vital for energy-dependent nations like India.
articles.raw_summary: Washington sees the Strait of Hormuz as exclusively an international waterway, whereas Tehran sees it as part of it territorial waters.

articles.title: How 50 days of the Iran war led to the loss of $50 billion worth of oil
articles.summary: The ongoing Iran conflict has disrupted global oil supplies, leading to the loss of over 500 million barrels of oil worth nearly $50 billion. Supply disruptions in the Gulf region have tightened oil inventories and reduced exports. Despite partial ceasefire signals and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, analysts expect a slow recovery with lasting impacts on energy markets.
articles.raw_summary: Global oil markets have lost over 500 million barrels since the Iran conflict began, worth nearly $50 billion. Supply disruptions across the Gulf have tightened inventories and hit exports sharply. Despite partial ceasefire signals and Strait of Hormuz reopening, analysts expect recovery to be slow, with lasting energy market shocks.

articles.title: From protests to paychecks: How state unrest triggered a wage rethink
articles.summary: The Indian government is setting a national floor for minimum wages, which will require states to adjust their own minimum wages accordingly. Once implemented, the monthly wage will be calculated as 26 times the final daily wage. This move aims to standardize wage structures across states and address wage disparities triggered by unrest. The policy will impact millions of workers and compel state governments to revise their wage policies.
articles.raw_summary: Once a national floor is set, states will be compelled to revise their minimum wages accordingly, as wages cannot be set below the national floor. The monthly payout will be 26 times the final daily wage.

articles.title: India drops proposal to mandate national ID app Aadhaar on smartphones after pushback
articles.summary: India's government has dropped a proposal to pre-install the Aadhaar app on smartphones. This decision follows opposition from smartphone makers like Apple and Samsung. The government previously withdrew a similar mandate for a telecom app.

articles.summary: A court in Madurai sentenced nine policemen to death for torturing and murdering Jayaraj and Benicks, a father-son duo, in Sattankulam. The victims were allegedly killed for violating COVID curfew hours, though the CBI investigation debunked the police's claim. The judgment highlights systemic issues in law enforcement and judicial accountability. The case underscores the need for reforms to prevent custodial violence and uphold civil liberties.

This move aligns with India's push for indigenous defence capabilities and reduced reliance on foreign maintenance support.

The ruling highlights the enforcement of anti-conversion laws in the state and their judicial interpretation.

articles.summary: A boat carrying over 250 Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea after departing from Bangladesh, with about 250 people missing. The tragedy highlights ongoing challenges in refugee migration and maritime safety in the region.

The agencies said the trawler carrying more than 250 men, women and children reportedly sank due to harsh weather and overcrowding. The situation raises concerns about potential disruptions to global oil shipments, which could affect India's energy imports and economic stability.

The blockade disrupts Iran's main source of foreign currency and could impact global oil supplies, raising concerns about energy security and geopolitical stability.

articles.title: Punjab’s border farmers caught between fence and Zero Line
articles.summary: The central government has initiated steps to relocate the border fencing closer to the international boundary in Punjab. This move is expected to provide relief to farmers who have long faced stringent regulations under the Border Security Force (BSF). The BSF has enforced strict controls for decades, impacting agricultural activities near the border. The relocation aims to ease these restrictions and improve conditions for border farmers.
articles.raw_summary: The Central government’s long-awaited recent move to relocate border fencing in Punjab closer to the international boundary with Pakistan may finally offer farmers the freedom they have awaited for decades. Trapped between the border fence and the Zero Line, farmers are hopeful that they will be able to access their fields freely, have more crop choices, and be free from restricted farming hours and frequent security checks. articles.title: Historic legacy of Parade Ground in Fort Kochi marred by years of neglect
articles.summary: The Parade Ground in Fort Kochi, once upgraded for the FIFA Under-17 World Cup matches in 2017, has seen years of neglect despite being one of the few open spaces available for sports enthusiasts in West Kochi. The ground, which serves as a crucial public infrastructure for local residents, has received little maintenance since the event. This neglect highlights broader urban governance challenges in maintaining public facilities in Kochi.

articles.title: Residents demand boost for small industries and water supply for agriculture in Jolarpet
articles.summary: Residents in Jolarpet, Tamil Nadu, have demanded government support to boost small-scale industries, particularly incense stick-making units employing around 40,000 people, mostly women. They also urged improved water supply for agricultural activities. The demands reflect local economic and livelihood concerns that may require policy intervention.

articles.title: Oil plunges, stocks jumps as Iran declares Hormuz open
articles.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi announced on X that the Strait of Hormuz is now fully open for commercial vessel passage for the remainder of the ceasefire period. The strait is a vital route for global oil shipments, including those to India. The announcement follows recent tensions in the region and could impact oil prices and supply chains, which are critical for India's energy and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on X that “passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open for the remaining period of ceasefire”

articles.title: Slight increase in unemployment rate in March
articles.summary: The overall Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) for persons aged 15 and above declined to 55.4% in March from 55.9% in February. The rural LFPR also fell to 58% from 58.7% in the same period. These figures highlight employment trends that could influence government policy decisions or welfare schemes aimed at addressing job market challenges.
articles.raw_summary: Overall Labour Force Participation Rate among persons aged 15 and above was 55.4% in March, lower than 55.9% in February. The rural LFPR was 58% in March compared to 58.7% in previous month.

articles.title: Wholesale price inflation rises 3.88% in March on surge in crude rates
articles.summary: Wholesale Price Index (WPI)-based inflation in India rose to 3.88% in March, up from 2.13% in February and 2.25% in March last year. The increase is attributed to a surge in crude oil prices. Rising inflation may prompt the government or the Reserve Bank of India to consider policy measures to stabilize prices and manage economic impact.
articles.raw_summary: Wholesale Price Index (WPI)-based inflation was 2.13%in the previous month and 2.25% in March last year

articles.title: IMF cuts West Asia, North Africa growth forecast to 1.1% over Iran war
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has reduced its growth forecast for West Asia and North Africa to 1.1% for this year, down from a January prediction of 3.9%, citing the impact of the Iran war. Iran, Iraq, and Qatar are expected to be particularly affected. The IMF warned that the conflict could disrupt regional stability and economic activity, which may have implications for global oil supplies and trade dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: Iran, Iraq and Qatar will be particularly hard-hit, the IMF’s World Economic Outlook warned, as it revised down its January prediction of 3.9% regional growth this year

articles.title: Crude oil demand to see biggest quarterly plunge since Covid: IEA
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) reported that global crude oil demand is expected to see its largest quarterly drop since the Covid-19 pandemic. Russia earned $19 billion last month from crude and oil product exports, which surged to 7.1 million barrels per day, up from just 320,000 barrels per day in February. The article highlights shifting global oil trade patterns that could impact energy markets and prices worldwide.
articles.raw_summary: Russia earned $19 billion last month as crude and oil product exports soared to 7.1 million barrels per day from just 320,000 barrels a day in February, the IEA said

articles.title: Fuel, fertiliser prices may remain high for prolonged period: IMF, World Bank, IEA
articles.summary: The IMF, World Bank, and IEA have warned that fuel and fertilizer prices may remain high for an extended period due to ongoing uncertainty in West Asia. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping route, has not yet normalized, affecting global supply chains. This situation could impact India's energy imports, inflation, and agricultural costs, potentially leading to policy discussions on price stabilization or alternative supply routes.
articles.raw_summary: The statement said the situation in West Asia remains very uncertain, and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is yet to normalise

articles.title: Oil jumps 8% to above $100 ahead of U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on April 12, 2026, that the U.S. Navy would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran failed to end the ongoing war. The Strait is a key route for global oil shipments, and a blockade could disrupt oil supplies and raise prices. This development has significant implications for India's energy security, trade relations, and foreign policy.

articles.title: YouTube rolls out option to turn off Shorts, expands time controls
articles.summary: YouTube has introduced a new feature allowing users to turn off Shorts entirely, including a zero-minute limit to hide short videos. The update also expands time controls for content consumption. The changes apply to all users, though supervised teens may face stricter restrictions. The feature is part of YouTube's broader efforts to manage screen time and content visibility.
articles.raw_summary: YouTube is rolling out a zero-minute Shorts limit that can hide short videos entirely. While teens under supervision face strict blocks, regular users can still bypass the restriction easily. articles.title: European Commission proposes Google have to share search data with rivals under the DMA
articles.summary: The European Commission has proposed that Google must share anonymized search data, including user queries, clicks, rankings, and search result pages, with competitors in the European Economic Area under the Digital Markets Act (DMA). This move aims to promote competition by allowing rivals access to critical data. The proposal is part of broader efforts to regulate large digital platforms. Google has not yet commented on the proposal.
articles.raw_summary: Under the Commission's proposal, Google would have to share a set of anonymised data including user queries, clicks, rankings and search result pages, with rivals in the European Economic Area The post European Commission proposes Google have to share search data with rivals under the DMA appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

articles.title: Fintechs leverage AI to boost creditworthiness and reduce rejections
articles.summary: Fintech startups like BankSathi, GoodScore, and Credgenics are using AI to help borrowers improve their credit scores and reduce loan rejections. This trend is particularly significant in smaller cities where access to formal credit is limited. AI automates much of the process, but manual intervention is still needed to resolve defaults with lenders.
articles.raw_summary: Fintech startups are leveraging AI to help borrowers improve their creditworthiness and reduce loan application rejections. Companies like BankSathi, GoodScore, and Credgenics are offering AI-led advisory services, addressing a significant demand, especially from smaller cities. While AI automates much of the process, manual intervention remains crucial for resolving defaults with lenders.

articles.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' during ceasefire
articles.summary: Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' during the current ceasefire period, as announced by Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi. This move aligns with the ceasefire in Lebanon. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital route for global oil shipments, including those bound for India, making this announcement significant for regional energy security.
articles.raw_summary: This opening is in effect for the remaining period of the current ceasefire. The announcement was made by Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi. This move aligns with the ceasefire in Lebanon.

articles.title: Did Iran agree to give US its nuclear material? Official slams Trump's 'non starter' demand
articles.summary: An Iranian official stated that Tehran has not agreed to transfer its highly enriched uranium stockpile abroad or relinquish control of its nuclear materials, contradicting a demand by former U.S. President Trump. The dispute highlights tensions over Iran's nuclear program, which could affect global energy markets and India's diplomatic relations, given Iran's significance as an oil supplier to India.
articles.raw_summary: According to the Iranian official, Tehran has not agreed to ship its stockpile of highly enriched uranium abroad or relinquish control of its nuclear materials.

articles.title: Don’t fear AI; it’s only aid, not substitute: CJI Surya Kant
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant advised judges to adopt artificial intelligence (AI) as a tool to assist, not replace, human decision-making in the judiciary. He emphasized that accuracy, ethics, and human judgment must remain central to the justice system. The remarks were made to encourage the use of AI while cautioning against over-reliance on technology.

articles.title: Mobility platforms and the new architecture of Indian cities
articles.summary: Indian cities are transforming. Mobility platforms are now essential urban infrastructure, connecting people and goods seamlessly. These platforms create income for drivers and provide valuable data for city planning. They are also key to adopting electric vehicles. This integrated approach is shaping more connected, accessible, and efficient cities for the future.

India could see around 66 GW of HVDC projects over time, with demand driven by grid expansion, data centres and rising electricity needs.

articles.title: MSME loans to face higher stress from West Asia conflict: Crisil
articles.summary: CRISIL Ratings warns that loans to India's micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) could face higher stress due to the ongoing West Asia conflict and the maturity of loans issued during a period of rapid growth. This could impact the financial health of MSMEs, which are a key driver of India's economy and employment.

articles.title: 502 structures to be destroyed as BMC begins major S V Road widening in Andheri
articles.summary: The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has begun a major road-widening project on S V Road in Andheri, Mumbai. Phase 1 involves demolishing 196 structures between J P Road junction and Ceasar Road junction, with only about 10 structures remaining. The project aims to improve infrastructure but will displace residents and businesses. The BMC is executing the work under its urban development mandate.
articles.raw_summary: Phase 1 alone accounts for 196 structures spanning the J P Road junction to the Ceasar Road junction. This phase is nearing completion, with only around 10 structures remaining.

The NCW, a government body, is taking direct action to address the issue, highlighting concerns over workplace safety and gender-based violence in corporate settings.
articles.raw_summary: The panel will visit the facility on April 17 to conduct an on-the-spot inquiry, examine the relevant circumstances, and submit a report within 10 working days.

Opposition parties had criticized delays in seeking these relaxations.

articles.summary: Thirteen years after India's biggest financial fraud—the Saradha scam—court proceedings remain stalled in West Bengal. The case, involving massive financial losses for thousands of investors, reflects broader challenges in India's legal and regulatory frameworks for financial crimes.

articles.title: So what is the real oil price right now?
articles.summary: The Iran crisis has created a divide between financial and physical oil markets.
